LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS BRIAN HURLEY AND KATHLEEN KUSEK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER OR NOT CROSS-DEFENDANTS WERE CONSPIRING TO DIVERT BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY TO FIREHOUSE FROM GS&H. A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT IS RAISED AS TO WHETHER HURLEY WAS INTENTIONALLY MISREPRESENTING THE TORANI ACCOUNT TO CROSS-COMPLAINANTS SO THE BUSINESS COULD BE DIVERTED TO FIREWOOD, CAUSING GS&H TO LOSE THAT BUSINESS AND THEREFORE BE DAMAGED. Muller, Esq. of Nixon Peabody, LLP, (650) 320-7746, appearing for Defendants and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied.
